=== PLAN ===
# COR24 Ecosystem Refactoring Saga

Migrate ~19 repos across 3 GitHub orgs into canonical sw-cor24-* names
under sw-embed. Strategy: rename/fork/clone+push, preserve history,
deprecate old repos, build portal site.

See docs/refactoring-plan.md for full details.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-trim-emulator [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-emulator to its scope. Work in ~/github/sw-embed/sw-cor24-emula...
  [x] 002-trim-assembler [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-assembler to assembler scope. Remove emulator internals, web app, components. Keep src/assembler.rs + tests. Add path dep on ../sw-cor24-emulator. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure tests pass.
  [x] 003-trim-web-assembler [legacy]: Trim web-sw-cor24-assembler to web app scope. Remove CLI, rust-to-cor24, emulator/assembler source. Keep src/app.rs, components/, src/wasm.rs, src/challenge.rs, styles/, index.html, Trunk.toml. Update Cargo.toml path deps to ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh. Verify trunk build works.
  [x] 004-trim-rust [legacy]: Trim sw-cor24-rust to rust-to-cor24/ contents. Remove everything else. Restructure as top-level crate. Add path deps on ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Mark wasm2cor24 as deprecated. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 005-fork-pcode [legacy]: Fork pv24a to sw-cor24-pcode under sw-embed. Move contents into vm/ subdir. Copy pa24r into assembler/ and pl24r into linker/. Set up Rust workspace. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure all tests pass. <-- YOU ARE HERE
  [ ] 006-fork-web-pcode [legacy]: Fork web-dv24r to web-sw-cor24-pcode under sw-embed. Update path deps to ../sw-cor24-pcode, ../sw-cor24-assembler, ../sw-cor24-emulator. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh.
  [ ] 007-fork-pascal [legacy]: Fork p24p to sw-cor24-pascal under sw-embed. Move contents into compiler/. Copy p24c into compiler-v2/ and pr24p into runtime/. Update paths to reference sw-cor24-pcode. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure tests pass.
  [ ] 008-fork-web-pascal [legacy]: Fork web-p24c to web-sw-cor24-pascal under sw-embed. Update path deps. Update Trunk.toml public_url. Add scripts/build-pages.sh and scripts/serve.sh.
  [ ] 009-fork-tinyc [legacy]: Fork tc24r to sw-cor24-tinyc under sw-embed. Update path deps to ../sw-cor24-assembler and ../sw-cor24-emulator. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 010-fork-web-tinyc [legacy]: Fork web-tc24r to web-sw-cor24-tinyc under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 011-fork-forth [legacy]: Fork tf24a to sw-cor24-forth under sw-embed. Update path deps. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 012-fork-web-forth [legacy]: Fork web-tf24a to web-sw-cor24-forth under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 013-fork-macrolisp [legacy]: Fork tml24c to sw-cor24-macrolisp under sw-embed. Update path deps. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 014-fork-web-macrolisp [legacy]: Fork web-tml24c to web-sw-cor24-macrolisp under sw-embed. Update path deps and Trunk.toml. Add scripts.
  [ ] 015-fork-fortran [legacy]: Fork f24a to sw-cor24-fortran under sw-embed. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 016-fork-apl [legacy]: Fork a24a to sw-cor24-apl under sw-embed. Add scripts/build.sh.
  [ ] 017-deprecate-old-repos [legacy]: Add deprecation notices to all old repo READMEs. Link to new canonical repos under sw-embed.
  [ ] 018-portal-site [legacy]: Build sw-cor24-project GitHub Pages site with ISA overview, tutorials, language references, architecture diagrams, and links to all web-sw-cor24-* live demos.
  [ ] 019-verify-sweep [legacy]: Final sweep — verify all repos build/test, all demos deploy, all cross-repo links updated, all Cargo.toml package names correct, migration-status.md marked complete.

=== CURRENT STEP: 005-fork-pcode [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Fork pv24a to sw-cor24-pcode under sw-embed. Move contents into vm/ subdir. Copy pa24r into assembler/ and pl24r into linker/. Set up Rust workspace. Add scripts/build.sh. Ensure all tests pass.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

Perfect! Let me create a summary of the exploration:

## pl24r Repository Exploration Summary

### Repository Overview
**pl24r** is a Rust-based p-code linker for the COR24 ISA. It's a language-agnostic CLI tool that combines multiple `.spc` (symbolic p-code assembler) text files into one merged `.spc` file.

### Git Remote
```
origin  git@github.com:softwarewrighter/pl24r.git
```

### Directory Structure
```
/Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/pl24r/
├── Cargo.toml              (v0.1.0, Rust 2024 edition, no dependencies)
├── Cargo.lock
├── README.md
├── CLAUDE.md
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs              (re-exports: linker, parser, symbols modules)
│   ├── main.rs             (CLI entry point with argument parsing)
│   ├── parser.rs           (~800 lines, parses .spc files into AST)
│   ├── symbols.rs          (~530 lines, symbol table & validation)
│   └── linker.rs           (~730 lines, merges modules & emits output)
├── tests/
│   ├── cli_integration.rs  (16 CLI tests: args, output, errors)
│   ├── pipeline_e2e.rs     (7 end-to-end tests with fixtures)
│   └── fixtures/
│       ├── runtime.spc, app.spc, legacy_app.spc
│       └── e2e_runtime.spc, e2e_app.spc
├── docs/
│   ├── agent-cas-wiki.md
│   ├── agent-instructions.txt
│   ├── feature-request-*.md files
│   └── research.txt
├── scripts/
│   └── pipeline.sh         (convenience script for full pipeline)
└── target/                 (build artifacts)
```

### Key Files & Functionality

#### **Cargo.toml**
- Package name: `pl24r`
- Version: 0.1.0
- Edition: 2024
- No external dependencies

#### **src/main.rs**
- CLI argument parsing (-o for output file, -v/--verbose for diagnostics, -h/--help)
- Reads and parses input .spc files
- Builds symbol table and validates cross-module references
- Links modules (reorders so main module comes first)
- Emits output to file or stdout

#### **src/parser.rs** (Main Parser)
- Parses `.spc` directives: `.module`, `.export`, `.extern`, `.endmodule`
- Parses code structures: `.proc`, `.global`, `.data`, `.const`
- Handles labels, instructions, comments
- Includes 17 unit tests covering all parsing scenarios

#### **src/symbols.rs** (Symbol Validation)
- Builds global symbol table across modules
- Detects duplicate symbol definitions
- Resolves `.extern` references
- Validates exactly one `main` procedure exists
- Warns on unused exports
- Includes 11 unit tests

#### **src/linker.rs** (Module Merging & Emission)
- Reorders modules so `main` module is first (VM starts at offset 0)
- Merges globals, data, constants, and procedures
- Handles duplicate globals (silently merged)
- Detects conflicting constants with different values
- Emits clean .spc output, preserving labels and comments
- Strips linker-only metadata (.module, .export, .extern, .endmodule)
- Includes 15 unit tests

### Usage
```bash
pl24r [OPTIONS] <file.spc>...

Options:
  -o <path>      Write output to file (default: stdout)
  -v, --verbose  Print diagnostics to stderr
  -h, --help     Show this help

# Example:
pl24r runtime.spc app.spc -o combined.spc
```

### Pipeline Context
```
HLL source(s) → compiler → .spc file(s) ─┐
Runtime .spc                              ├→ pl24r → combined .spc → pasm → .p24 → COR24 VM
Library .spc modules                      ┘
```

### Testing
- **23 unit tests** integrated in source files (parser, symbols, linker modules)
- **16 CLI integration tests** in `tests/cli_integration.rs`
- **7 end-to-end tests** in `tests/pipeline_e2e.rs`
- Test fixtures include realistic pr24p runtime and app modules

### Related Projects
- [p24p](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/p24p) — Pascal compiler targeting COR24
- [pr24p](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/pr24p) — Pascal runtime library
- [pv24a](https://github.com/sw-vibe-coding/pv24a) — P-code VM and pasm assembler

## Summary: pa24r Repository

**Location:** `/Users/mike/github/softwarewrighter/pa24r`  
**Git Remote:** `git@github.com:softwarewrighter/pa24r.git`

### Project Overview
**pa24r** is a Rust-native two-pass p-code assembler for the COR24 toolchain. It reads linked `.spc` (symbolic p-code) source files and produces `.p24` binary files for execution on the pvm.s virtual machine.

**Pipeline Position:**
```
Pascal source -> p24p (compile) -> .spc -> pl24r (link) -> pa24r (assemble) -> .p24 -> pvm.s (execute)
```

### Directory Structure
```
pa24r/
├── Cargo.toml                  # Rust package configuration
├── Cargo.lock                  # Dependency lock file
├── README.md                   # Project documentation
├── CLAUDE.md                   # Claude-specific documentation
├── docs/
│   ├── agent-cas-wiki.md
│   ├── agent-instructions.txt
│   └── research.txt
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs                  # Main library (1,062 lines)
│   ├── main.rs                 # CLI entry point (136 lines)
│   └── lexer.rs                # Tokenizer (417 lines)
├── tests/
│   └── integration.rs          # Integration tests (289 lines)
└── target/                     # Build artifacts
```

### Key Files

**Cargo.toml:**
- Package name: `pa24r`
- Version: `0.1.0`
- Edition: 2024
- No external dependencies (pure Rust)

**src/lib.rs** defines:
- `Opcode` enum: 40+ p-code instructions (halt, push, arithmetic, logic, comparison, control flow, memory access, system calls)
- `Encoding` enum: 5 instruction encoding formats (None, Imm8, Imm24, D8A24, D8O8)
- `AssemblyResult` struct: code, data, entry_point, global_count, errors
- `AssemblyError` struct: line-number-aware error reporting
- `LoadedImage` struct: representation of loaded .p24 binaries
- `LoadError` enum: binary loading errors
- Core assembly logic with symbol tracking (Code, Data, Global, Const)

**src/main.rs** implements:
- CLI argument parsing (-o/--output required, -v/--verbose, --dump for hex output)
- File I/O and error handling
- Optional hex dump function for binary inspection

**src/lexer.rs** provides:
- `Token` enum: tokenization of .spc syntax
- `Located` struct: tokens with line number tracking
- `tokenize()` function: handles comments, negative numbers, labels, directives, commas

**tests/integration.rs** contains:
- 20 integration tests covering hello world, arithmetic, globals, loops, stack operations, comparisons, memory access, nested calls, bitwise operations, and trap handling
- Round-trip tests to verify assemble→load→verify cycle
- Error handling tests for malformed .p24 files

### .p24 Binary Format
18-byte header followed by code and data:
- 0x00-0x03: Magic (`P24\0`)
- 0x04: Version (1)
- 0x05-0x07: Entry point (24-bit LE)
- 0x08-0x0A: Code size (24-bit LE)
- 0x0B-0x0D: Data size (24-bit LE)
- 0x0E-0x10: Global count (24-bit LE)
- 0x11: Flags (0x00)

### Public API
```rust
assemble(source) -> AssemblyResult
assemble_to_p24(source) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Vec<AssemblyError>>
load_p24(binary) -> Result<LoadedImage, LoadError>
relocate_data_refs(&mut code, code_size, data_size, load_addr)
```

### Build & Test
```bash
cargo build
cargo test                     # 52 tests (32 unit + 20 integration)
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
```

### Related Projects
- **pv24a:** P-code VM reference implementation
- **web-dv24r:** Browser-based p-code debugger
- **p24p:** Pascal compiler (produces .spc)
- **pl24r:** Text-level linker
- **pr24p:** Pascal runtime library

├── README.md                   # Project documentation

├── docs/

├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs                  # Main library (1,062 lines)
│   ├── main.rs                 # CLI entry point (136 lines)
│   └── lexer.rs                # Tokenizer (417 lines)
├── tests/
│   └── integration.rs          # Integration tests (289 lines)
└── target/                     # Build artifacts
```

### Key Files

**Cargo.toml:**
- Package name: `pa24r`
- Version: `0.1.0`
- Edition: 2024
- No external dependencies (pure Rust)

**src/lib.rs** defines:
- `Opcode` enum: 40+ p-code instructions (halt, push, arithmetic, logic, comparison, control flow, memory access, system calls)
- `Encoding` enum: 5 instruction encoding formats (None, Imm8, Imm24, D8A24, D8O8)
- `AssemblyResult` struct: code, data, entry_point, global_count, errors
- `AssemblyError` struct: line-number-aware error reporting
- `LoadedImage` struct: representation of loaded .p24 binaries
- `LoadError` enum: binary loading errors
- Core assembly logic with symbol tracking (Code, Data, Global, Const)

**src/main.rs** implements:
- CLI argument parsing (-o/--output required, -v/--verbose, --dump for hex output)
- File I/O and error handling
- Optional hex dump function for binary inspection

**src/lexer.rs** provides:
- `Token` enum: tokenization of .spc syntax
- `Located` struct: tokens with line number tracking
- `tokenize()` function: handles comments, negative numbers, labels, directives, commas

**tests/integration.rs** contains:
- 20 integration tests covering hello world, arithmetic, globals, loops, stack operations, comparisons, memory access, nested calls, bitwise operations, and trap handling
- Round-trip tests to verify assemble→load→verify cycle
- Error handling tests for malformed .p24 files

### .p24 Binary Format
18-byte header followed by code and data:
- 0x00-0x03: Magic (`P24\0`)
- 0x04: Version (1)
- 0x05-0x07: Entry point (24-bit LE)
- 0x08-0x0A: Code size (24-bit LE)
- 0x0B-0x0D: Data size (24-bit LE)
- 0x0E-0x10: Global count (24-bit LE)
- 0x11: Flags (0x00)

### Public API
```rust
assemble(source) -> AssemblyResult
assemble_to_p24(source) -> Result<Vec<u8>, Vec<AssemblyError>>
load_p24(binary) -> Result<LoadedImage, LoadError>
relocate_data_refs(&mut code, code_size, data_size, load_addr)
```

cargo build
cargo test                     # 52 tests (32 unit + 20 integration)
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
```

- **web-dv24r:** Browser-based p-code debugger

- **pl24r:** Text-level linker

### Key Files

**pvm.s (2,201 lines)** - P-code VM Interpreter
- Register allocation: r0=W (work), r1/r2=scratch, fp=base, sp=hardware stack
- VM state struct (vm_state) with 9 fields: pc, esp, csp, fp_vm, gp, hp, code, status, trap_code
- 30+ opcodes (stack, arithmetic, bitwise, comparison, control flow, procedures, memory access)
- UART helpers for I/O (uart_putc, uart_puts at -65280/-65279)
- Trap handling, dynamic binary loading support
- Built-in test bytecode and self-test routines

**pasm.s (2,716 lines)** - P-Code Assembler
- Two-pass assembler: lexical analysis → symbol collection → bytecode emission
- Token types: EOF, newline, number, identifier, directive, label, comma
- Symbol table: constants, labels, procedures, globals, data blocks
- Operand encoding: NONE (1-byte), IMM8 (2-byte), IMM24 (4-byte), D8_A24 (5-byte), D8_O8 (3-byte)
- Reads from UART, outputs bytecode to standard output

**pvmasm.s (5,347 lines)** - Integrated Assembler + VM
- Combines pasm and pvm in one executable
- Assembles .spc source on-the-fly, then executes resulting bytecode
- Single-step workflow: source → bytecode → execution

**demo.sh** - Test Harness
- Runs 12 golden-output tests with color-coded results (PASS/FAIL/SKIP)
- Test categories: core ops, stack/logic, memory/procedures, advanced, data directives, UART I/O, edge cases, linked programs
- Supports single-file runner: `./demo.sh run FILE.spc`
- Syntax: `./demo.sh [test|run FILE.spc]`

### Instruction Set Summary
**Stack**: push, push_s, dup, drop, swap, over
**Arithmetic**: add, sub, mul, div, mod, neg
**Bitwise**: and, or, xor, not, shl, shr
**Comparison**: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge
**Control Flow**: jmp, jz, jnz, call, calln, ret, halt, trap
**Memory**: loadl, storel, loada, storea, loadg, storeg, loadn, storen, load, store, loadb, storeb
**System**: enter, leave (frame management), sys (system calls: HALT, PUTC, GETC, LED, ALLOC, FREE, READ_SWITCH)

### Example P-Code (.spc)
The `.spc` format is a simple assembly-like syntax:
```
.data msg 72, 101, 108, 108, 111, 10, 0
.proc main 0
    push msg
    call puts
    halt
.end
.proc puts 1
    loada 0
    storel 0
loop:
    loadl 0
    loadb
    dup
    jz done
    sys 1        # PUTC system call
    loadl 0
    push 1
    add
    storel 0
    jmp loop
done:
    drop
    ret 1
.end
```

- **Fully functional** with end-to-end execution verification

- Supports linked multi-module programs
- External binary loading via `--load-binary` and `--patch` flags
- MIT licensed (Copyright 2026 Michael A Wright)

- tc24r — Tiny C compiler for COR24 (Rust)
- tml24c — Tiny Macro Lisp for COR24
- cor24-rs — COR24 assembler and emulator
- COR24-TB — COR24 FPGA target board

## 4. The cor24-rs Split

`cor24-rs` is a Rust workspace with tightly coupled crates. It becomes
**four** new repos via rename + fork + clone+push. All four preserve
the full `cor24-rs` git history, then each is trimmed to its scope.

**Method:**
1. **Rename** `cor24-rs` → `sw-cor24-emulator` (primary successor)
2. **Fork** `sw-cor24-emulator` → `sw-cor24-assembler` (gets fork badge)
3. **Clone+push** → `web-sw-cor24-assembler`
4. **Clone+push** → `sw-cor24-rust`

GitHub auto-redirects `sw-embed/cor24-rs` → `sw-embed/sw-cor24-emulator`
permanently. The fork slot is used for the assembler (most closely related).
The remaining two get full history via clone+push (no fork badge).

```
cor24-rs/
├── Cargo.toml          # workspace: [".", "components", "cli", "isa"]
├── isa/                # cor24-isa — ISA definitions (shared, no deps)
├── src/
│   ├── assembler.rs    # 52KB — full assembler

# pv24a -- P-Code VM for COR24

A p-code virtual machine and p-code assembler (pasm), both written in COR24 assembly. pv24a provides a language-neutral stack-based execution substrate for compilers (Pascal, Lisp, BASIC, etc.) that emit p-code bytecode.

Runs on the COR24 emulator ([cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs)) and eventually on FPGA hardware ([COR24-TB](https://makerlisp.com)).

## Project Status

The VM and assembler are **fully functional** with end-to-end execution: `.spc` source → p-code bytecode → VM execution, all verified by a 12-test suite with golden output comparison (including linked multi-module programs).

### Components

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pvm.s` | P-code VM interpreter — 30+ opcodes, trap handling, dynamic binary loading |
| `pasm.s` | Standalone two-pass p-code assembler |
| `pvmasm.s` | Integrated assembler+VM — assembles `.spc` source and executes in one step |
| `demo.sh` | Test harness with 12 golden-output tests and single-file runner |

### Instruction Set

- **Stack**: push, push_s, dup, drop, swap, over
- **Arithmetic**: add, sub, mul, div, mod, neg
- **Bitwise**: and, or, xor, not, shl, shr
- **Comparison**: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge (push 1/0)
- **Control flow**: jmp, jz, jnz
- **Procedures**: call, calln, ret, enter, leave
- **Locals/args**: loadl, storel, loada, storea
- **Globals**: loadg, storeg, addrg, addrl
- **Nonlocal access**: loadn, storen (static link chain for nested Pascal procedures)
- **Indirect memory**: load, store, loadb, storeb
- **System calls**: HALT, PUTC, GETC, LED, ALLOC (bump allocator), FREE, READ_SWITCH

## Related

- [tf24a](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tf24a) -- DTC Forth in COR24 assembly
- [tc24r](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tc24r) -- Tiny C compiler for COR24 (Rust)
- [tml24c](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tml24c) -- Tiny Macro Lisp for COR24
- [cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs) -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
- [COR24-TB](https://makerlisp.com) -- The COR24 FPGA target board

## Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Design](docs/design.md) | VM spec: opcodes, encoding, call frame layout, pasm syntax |
| [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) | System layers, memory map, register allocation |
| [PRD](docs/prd.md) | Product requirements, deliverables, success criteria |
| [Research](docs/research.txt) | Deep research on p-code VM design and implementation approach |

## Building and Running

Requires [cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs) (`cor24-run` binary).

```bash
# Run the test suite (12 tests with golden output comparison)
./demo.sh

# Assemble and run a single .spc file via the integrated assembler+VM
./demo.sh run hello.spc

# Run the VM with its embedded test bytecode
cor24-run --run pvm.s --speed 0

# Assemble .spc source directly via pvmasm.s (assembles + executes)
cor24-run --run pvmasm.s -u "$(cat hello.spc)"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n 20000000

# Dump CPU state or trace execution
cor24-run --run pvm.s --dump --speed 0
cor24-run --run pvm.s --trace 100 --speed 0
```

### Examples

See `examples/` for sample `.spc` programs and `tests/` for the test suite inputs.

## COR24 Architecture at a Glance

- **24-bit** registers and address space (16 MB)
- **3 GPRs**: r0, r1, r2 (plus fp, sp, z)
- **Variable-length** instruction encoding (1/2/4 bytes)
- Hardware multiply, no hardware divide
- Little-endian, byte-addressable
- UART and LED via memory-mapped I/O

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

Copyright (c) 2026 Michael A Wright

# sw-cor24-pcode -- P-Code Tools for COR24

A Rust workspace containing the p-code toolchain for the COR24 ISA:

| Directory | What | Impl |
|-----------|------|------|
| `vm/` | P-code VM + standalone assembler (pasm) | COR24 assembly |
| `assembler/` | P-code binary assembler (.spc -> .p24) | Rust |
| `linker/` | P-code linker (merges .spc modules) | Rust |

## Pipeline

```

# pv24a -- P-Code VM for COR24

A p-code virtual machine and p-code assembler (pasm), both written in COR24 assembly. pv24a provides a language-neutral stack-based execution substrate for compilers (Pascal, Lisp, BASIC, etc.) that emit p-code bytecode.

Runs on the COR24 emulator ([cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs)) and eventually on FPGA hardware ([COR24-TB](https://makerlisp.com)).

## Project Status

The VM and assembler are **fully functional** with end-to-end execution: `.spc` source → p-code bytecode → VM execution, all verified by a 12-test suite with golden output comparison (including linked multi-module programs).

### Components

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `pvm.s` | P-code VM interpreter — 30+ opcodes, trap handling, dynamic binary loading |
| `pasm.s` | Standalone two-pass p-code assembler |
| `pvmasm.s` | Integrated assembler+VM — assembles `.spc` source and executes in one step |
| `demo.sh` | Test harness with 12 golden-output tests and single-file runner |

### Instruction Set

- **Stack**: push, push_s, dup, drop, swap, over
- **Arithmetic**: add, sub, mul, div, mod, neg
- **Bitwise**: and, or, xor, not, shl, shr
- **Comparison**: eq, ne, lt, le, gt, ge (push 1/0)
- **Control flow**: jmp, jz, jnz
- **Procedures**: call, calln, ret, enter, leave
- **Locals/args**: loadl, storel, loada, storea
- **Globals**: loadg, storeg, addrg, addrl
- **Nonlocal access**: loadn, storen (static link chain for nested Pascal procedures)
- **Indirect memory**: load, store, loadb, storeb
- **System calls**: HALT, PUTC, GETC, LED, ALLOC (bump allocator), FREE, READ_SWITCH

## Related

- [tf24a](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tf24a) -- DTC Forth in COR24 assembly
- [tc24r](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tc24r) -- Tiny C compiler for COR24 (Rust)
- [tml24c](https://github.com/softwarewrighter/tml24c) -- Tiny Macro Lisp for COR24
- [cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs) -- COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust)
- [COR24-TB](https://makerlisp.com) -- The COR24 FPGA target board

## Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Design](docs/design.md) | VM spec: opcodes, encoding, call frame layout, pasm syntax |
| [Architecture](docs/architecture.md) | System layers, memory map, register allocation |
| [PRD](docs/prd.md) | Product requirements, deliverables, success criteria |
| [Research](docs/research.txt) | Deep research on p-code VM design and implementation approach |

## Building and Running

Requires [cor24-rs](https://github.com/sw-embed/cor24-rs) (`cor24-run` binary).

```bash
# Run the test suite (12 tests with golden output comparison)
./demo.sh

# Assemble and run a single .spc file via the integrated assembler+VM
./demo.sh run hello.spc

# Run the VM with its embedded test bytecode
cor24-run --run pvm.s --speed 0

# Assemble .spc source directly via pvmasm.s (assembles + executes)
cor24-run --run pvmasm.s -u "$(cat hello.spc)"$'\x04' --speed 0 -n 20000000

# Dump CPU state or trace execution
cor24-run --run pvm.s --dump --speed 0
cor24-run --run pvm.s --trace 100 --speed 0
```

### Examples

See `examples/` for sample `.spc` programs and `tests/` for the test suite inputs.

## COR24 Architecture at a Glance

- **24-bit** registers and address space (16 MB)
- **3 GPRs**: r0, r1, r2 (plus fp, sp, z)
- **Variable-length** instruction encoding (1/2/4 bytes)
- Hardware multiply, no hardware divide
- Little-endian, byte-addressable
- UART and LED via memory-mapped I/O

## License

MIT -- see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

Copyright (c) 2026 Michael A Wright

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## CRITICAL: AgentRail Session Protocol (MUST follow exactly)

This project uses AgentRail. Every session follows this exact sequence:

### 1. START (do this FIRST, before anything else)
```bash
agentrail next
```
Read the output carefully. It tells you your current step, prompt, skill docs, and past trajectories.

### 2. BEGIN (immediately after reading the next output)
```bash
agentrail begin
```

### 3. WORK (do what the step prompt says)
Do NOT ask the user "want me to proceed?" or "shall I start?". The step prompt IS your instruction. Execute it.

### 4. COMMIT (after the work is done)
Commit your code changes with git.

### 5. COMPLETE (LAST thing, after committing)
```bash
agentrail complete --summary "what you accomplished" \
  --reward 1 \
  --actions "tools and approach used"
```
If the step failed: `--reward -1 --failure-mode "what went wrong"`
If the saga is finished: add `--done`

### 6. STOP (after complete, DO NOT continue working)
Do NOT make any further code changes after running agentrail complete.
Any changes after complete are untracked and invisible to the next session.
If you see more work to do, it belongs in the NEXT step, not this session.

Do NOT skip any of these steps. The next session depends on your trajectory recording.

## Project: pv24a — P-Code VM for COR24 ISA

A p-code virtual machine and p-code assembler (pasm), both written in COR24
assembly (.s files). The Pascal compiler is a **separate project** — this
project only implements the VM and the assembler used to test it.

## File Extensions

- `.s` — COR24 assembly source (VM interpreter, pasm assembler)
- `.spc` — P-code assembler source (input to pasm)
- `.p24` — Assembled p-code bytecode (output of pasm, input to VM)

## Key Documentation (READ BEFORE WORKING)

- `docs/research.txt` — Deep research on p-code VM design, memory model, calling conventions, instruction set, and p-code assembler design. Source of truth for design rationale.
- `docs/design.md` — VM specification: opcodes, encoding table, call frame layout, calling convention, pasm syntax, trap codes, memory segments.
- `docs/architecture.md` — Layered system architecture, memory map, register allocation, component map.
- `docs/prd.md` — Product requirements, deliverables, success criteria.

## Agent Wiki (Multi-Agent Coordination)

This project participates in a shared wiki for cross-component coordination.
See `docs/agent-cas-wiki.md` for the full API reference and CAS protocol.

```bash
# List all wiki pages
curl -s http://localhost:7402/api/pages

# Read a page
curl -s http://localhost:7402/api/pages/PV24A

# Key pages to check each session:
#   AgentStatus          — who's online, what they're doing
#   AgentToAgentRequests — cross-component dependency requests
#   COR24Toolchain       — overall toolchain coordination
#   P24Toolchain         — pipeline overview and design decisions
#   PV24A                — this project's wiki page
```

**Use PATCH for edits** — PATCH makes surgical line-level changes (replace, append_after,
insert_before, delete) and avoids overwriting other agents' work. Use PUT only for creating
new pages. Always include the ETag. See `docs/agent-cas-wiki.md` for full API reference.

## Related Projects

- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/tf24a` — DTC Forth in COR24 assembly (pattern reference for .s project structure, testing, CLAUDE.md)
- `~/github/sw-embed/cor24-rs` — COR24 assembler and emulator (Rust, provides `cor24-run`)
- `~/github/sw-vibe-coding/agentrail-domain-coding` — Coding skills domain

## Available Task Types

`cor24-asm`, `pre-commit`

## Build & Test

```bash
# Assemble and run a .s file
cor24-run --run <file.s> --speed 0

# With UART input
cor24-run --run <file.s> -u 'input text\n' --speed 0 -n 5000000

# Dump CPU state after halt
cor24-run --run <file.s> --dump --speed 0

# Trace last N instructions
cor24-run --run <file.s> --trace 100 --speed 0

# Interactive REPL mode
cor24-run --run <file.s> --terminal --echo
```

## COR24 ISA Constraints (MUST follow)

- **3 GPRs only**: r0, r1, r2 (loads/ALU destination)
- **fp**: only usable as base register for indexed memory ops
- **sp**: hardware stack in EBR, cannot receive load results
- **Cell size**: 3 bytes (24-bit words)
- **Labels**: must be on their own line (`label:` not `label: instr`)
- **Comments**: semicolon only (`;`)
- **No string literals**: use `.byte 72, 101, 108, 108, 111` for "Hello"
- **No hex in assembler**: use decimal (`-65280` not `0xFF0100`)
- **Branch offset**: ±127 bytes; far jumps: `la r0, label; jmp (r0)`
- **`.word label`**: emits 24-bit address (one label per directive)

## Register Allocation (for VM interpreter)

```
r0 = W (work/scratch, opcode dispatch)
r1 = VM state pointer or secondary scratch
r2 = scratch
sp = COR24 hardware stack (EBR, use sparingly)
fp = memory base for indexed loads/stores
```

## UART I/O

- Data register: -65280 (0xFF0100)
- Status register: -65279 (0xFF0101)
- TX busy: bit 7 (sign bit when sign-extended via `lb`)
- RX ready: bit 0
- LED/Switch port: -65536 (0xFF0000)

# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---
